# authorship-strategy-skill

> A Claude Code skill (documentation-only, MIT, v0.1.0 Unreleased) that loads the four-layer judgment framework for *authorship strategy under AI-mediated diffusion* into an LLM-based coding agent's rule set. Operational form of the [`authorship-strategy`](https://github.com/shimo4228/authorship-strategy) research line. Three-axis inversion (scarcity → diffusion, exclusivity → derivation, enclosure → openness) + four-layer judgment stack (Authenticity → Attribution Diffusion → Idea vs Scaffold → Tactics). Single-author normative; applies only to the author's own DOI-targeted idea-rescue research repositories.

## Core documentation

- [SKILL.md](skills/authorship-strategy/SKILL.md): full skill specification — trigger conditions, three-axis inversion as core principle, four-layer judgment framework, fourteen-item judgment checklist, prohibited and encouraged actions, scope-limiting conditions
- [README.md](README.md): human-facing narrative (when to use, install, how it works, key concept)
- [CHANGELOG.md](CHANGELOG.md): release notes

## Trigger scope

The skill applies when *all* of the following hold:

- Repository owner is the skill's adopter (single-author normative)
- Repository is a DOI-targeted or DOI-registered research artifact (Zenodo or comparable archive registration intended or in place)
- Artifact is idea-rescue in character (specifications, schemas, ADRs, glossaries, doctrine; not implementation or scaffolding)

Skips for client work, contributions to others' projects, scaffold-only tooling, day-to-day coding.

## Key concept

The three axes are *not independent*: a strategy mixing axes (enclosure with derivation-welcoming license; scarcity with crawler-friendly access) is internally inconsistent and weaker than either consistent strategy. The skill enforces axis consistency: tactical decisions violating any axis are flagged regardless of local attractiveness.

## Doctrine repository

- [authorship-strategy](https://github.com/shimo4228/authorship-strategy): the normative framework, thirteen tactical ADRs, empirical baseline, glossary, manifesto

## Peer components

Other component skills of the same framework:

- [release-doi](https://github.com/shimo4228/release-doi): release-time workflow operationalizing the framework's identifier-federation triplet (ADRs 0001-0003) and the intrinsic-identifier archival step (ADR-0013)
- [llms-txt-writer](https://github.com/shimo4228/llms-txt-writer): operationalizes Layer 4 tactic 7's Answer.AI `llms.txt` convention
- [jsonld-knowledge-graph](https://github.com/shimo4228/jsonld-knowledge-graph): operationalizes Layer 4 tactic 7's JSON-LD knowledge graph

Terminology: *sibling* is reserved for research-line-level peers; at the component-skill level the term *peer component* is used.

## Related ecosystem skills

- [llms-txt-writer](https://github.com/shimo4228/llms-txt-writer): AI-facing documentation prose
- [jsonld-knowledge-graph](https://github.com/shimo4228/jsonld-knowledge-graph): JSON-LD knowledge graph design
- [context-sync](https://github.com/shimo4228/context-sync): cross-document drift audits
- [claude-skill-writing-ecosystem](https://github.com/shimo4228/claude-skill-writing-ecosystem): human-facing writing orchestrator

## Sibling research lines

The framework is one of five DOI-registered research lines in the author's research program:

- [authorship-strategy](https://github.com/shimo4228/authorship-strategy)
- [agent-knowledge-cycle (AKC)](https://github.com/shimo4228/agent-knowledge-cycle) — DOI 10.5281/zenodo.19200726
- [contemplative-agent](https://github.com/shimo4228/contemplative-agent) — DOI 10.5281/zenodo.19212118
- [agent-attribution-practice (AAP)](https://github.com/shimo4228/agent-attribution-practice) — DOI 10.5281/zenodo.19652013
- [attention-not-self](https://github.com/shimo4228/attention-not-self) — DOI 10.5281/zenodo.20262112
